rulings.Case Title: DAVID GUNN VS. GLOBAL GOURMET CATERING, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion For An Order Determining Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 2, 2024, Line 1, DEFENDANT ALAN HONNIBALL's Motion For An Order Determining Good Faith Settlement. Defendant Alan Honniball's unopposed Motion for Order Determining Good Faith Settlement is granted. Specifically, the settlement between Plaintiff Gunn and Defendant Alan Honniball individually and dba Main Act Productions, attached to the declaration of Holly A. Graves, was made in good faith pursuant to CCP section 877.6. Further, all other defendants and parties are barred from pursuing a claim against Defendant Alan Honniball individually and dba Main Act Productions for equitable contribution, or partial or comparative indemnity, based on comparative negligence or comparative fault in this case. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RCE).

rulings.Case Title: JELANI DOTSON VS. BRIDGE HOUSING CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 2, 2024, Line 5, DEFENDANT BRIDGE HOUSING CORPORATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. The matter is Off calendar as duplicate. The notice of errata filed April 24, 2024, clarifies that plaintiff's employer is BRIDGE Property Management Company (BPMC), but it was erroneously sued as Bridge Housing Corporation. BPMC's motion for summary judgment filed on April 26, 2024, is thus the operative motion. =(302/RCE).

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SPALDING VS. VAN NESS HAYES ASSOCIATES LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Holding Plaintiff Jennifer Spalding In Contempt For Failure To Provide Full Supplemental Responses To Defendants Form Interrogatories, For An Order Of Further Monetary Sanctions Totaling $3,595.00; And For An Order For Issue Sanctions, And Evidentiary Sanctions, Pursuant To C.C.P. 2023.030
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, August 2, 2024, Line 2, 1- DEFENDANT EMERALD FUND INC.'s Motion For An Order Holding Plaintiff Jennifer Spalding In Contempt For Failure To Provide Full Supplemental Responses To Defendants Form Interrogatories, For An Order Of Further Monetary Sanctions Totaling $3,595.00; And For An Order For Issue Sanctions, And Evidentiary Sanctions, Pursuant To C.C.P. 2023.030. Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted in part. A detailed Tentative Ruling has been emailed to the parties. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPARTMENT 525 DAILY AT 9:00 A.M.), or dial the corresponding number and use the meeting ID, and password for Discovery Department 525. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ELIAS CHAHWAN VS. JOHN COMPAGNO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion Of Defendant John Compagno To Quash Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, August 2, 2024, Line 3, 2- DEFENDANT JOHN COMPAGNO's Motion to Quash Subpoena. (Part 1 of 2 for purposes of entry of Tentative Ruling.) Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Compagno moves to quash a subpoena addressed to Holland & Knight LLP and related notice on grounds the same are allegedly untimely and fail to satisfy the notice requirements of CCP section 1985.3. The file sought by the subpoena pertains to legal representation of Plaintiff Chahwan by Defendant Compagno as a partner in Holland & Knight. Compagno claims that the file contains personal records pertaining to him, that he is a consumer because he transacted business with Holland & Knight, and that he did not receive a consumer records notice pursuant to CCP section 1985.3(e). Defendant's motion requires the assumptions that a lawyer can be a consumer vis a vis his former client, that Compagno, a law partner, "transact[ed] business" with his law firm, that Chahwan's client file contains personal records of Compagno as responsible attorney, that any rights in Compagno's personal records were not waived to the extent they are part of Chahwan's client file, and that the file in question does not already belong to Plaintiff Chahwan under Rule of Professional Conduct 1.16(e)(1) and related authorities. These assumptions lack evidentiary or legal support. The Motion to Quash must be denied. The Court finds that Defendant's motion to quash was without substantial justification. Accordingly, Defendant Compagno and his attorneys are ordered to pay sanctions of $1500 to Plaintiff Chahwan on or before August 9, 2024. (Tentative Ruling continues in Part 2 of 2) =(JPT)

If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Compagno moves to stay the deposition of Cristina Barring and to quash the Notice of Deposition Subpoena for Personal Appearance of Cristina Barring on the grounds that the Notice was untimely and fails to satisfy the notice requirements set forth in CCP sec. 2025.270. Plaintiff Chahwan contends that the deposition subpoena was served on a date allowing a reasonable time for the witness to travel to the place of the deposition under CCP sec. 2020.220(a) such that no deposition notice was necessary to compel the witness to travel to attend. Plaintiff's argument misconceives the statutory scheme for taking depositions and compelling the attendance of witnesses and further seeks to create a conflict between Sections 2020.220 and 2025.270 when none exists. Service of a deposition notice is the foundational step in taking a deposition. CCP secs. 2025.210 and 2025.220(a). An oral deposition must be scheduled for a date at least 10 days after service of the deposition notice, subject to a motion or ex parte application to shorten or extend time. CCP sec. 2025.270. If a subpoena is required to compel appearance of the witness, it must be served in adequate time for the witness to travel to the place of deposition. CCP sec. 2020.220(a). Plaintiff cites no authority that compliance with Section 2020.220 trumps the requirements of section 2025.270. The opposite is true; both sections must be obeyed. Notice of litigation proceedings is an obvious requirement of due process of law. Plaintiff's attempt to avoid the notice provisions of Section 2020.270 must fail. The motion to quash notice of the Barring deposition is granted and the deposition of Barring is stayed, subject to renotice in compliance with CCP sec. 2025.270. The Court finds that Plaintiff's opposition to the motion to quash was without substantial justification. Accordingly, Plaintiff Chahwan and his attorneys are ordered to pay sanctions of $1500 Defendant Compagno on or before August 9, 2024. (Tentative Ruling continues in Part 2 of 2) =(JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. LASTING IMPRESSIONS DENTAL GROUP, PLLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 2, 2024, Line 9, PLAINTIFF ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC.'s, Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order. Plaintiff Align Technology's unopposed motion for Assignment of Rights, Restraining Order, and Turnover Order is granted. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RCE).

rulings.Case Title: STUDENT LOAN SOLUTIONS, LLC VS. CURTIS U COOPER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 2, 2024, Line 10, CROSS DEFENDANT STUDENT LOAN SOLUTIONS, LLC, CHRISTOPHER RUH, GOLDSMITH & HULL, APC's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT. The matter is taken off calendar as Student Loan Solutions' anti-SLAPP motion is moot. Cross-Defendant SLS brought the ex parte motion to reclassify the First Amended Cross-Complaint which resulted in the parties' agreement, and a court order, to file an amended cross complaint (see order signed July 18, 2024). The Second Amended Cross Complaint was filed by Cross-Plaintiff Cooper. It is now the controlling pleading and supersedes the original one, which now has no function. (Foreman & Clark Corp. v. Fallon, 3 Cal.3d. 875, 884.) Accordingly, the anti-SLAPP motion directed to the First Amended Cross-Complaint is moot. =(302/RCE).
